false_form: The natural proofs barrier (Razborov-Rudich 1997) rules out all known combinatorial lower bound techniques for separating complexity classes.
name: NATURAL_PROOFS_UNIVERSALITY
true_form: The natural proofs barrier (Razborov-Rudich 1997) strictly rules out proof techniques that simultaneously satisfy formal constructivity (the predicate is computable in time 2^O(n)) AND largeness (it accepts a non-negligible fraction of all functions), assuming strong pseudorandom generators exist. Bounds against weaker classes such as AC^0 are not constructive in the relevant sense and bypass it; bounds via non-constructive properties (e.g. counting or measure-theoretic arguments) also bypass it. The algebraic natural-proofs analogue (Forbes-Shpilka-Volk 2017, Grochow-Kumar-Saks-Saraf 2017) is conjectural, not proven.
verification_source: Gemini Deep Research pilot DR-S001 2026-05-14; primary cite Razborov-Rudich 1997 JCSS 55(1):24-35 (DOI 10.1006/jcss.1997.1494); algebraic-natural-proofs follow-on Forbes-Shpilka-Volk 2017 (arXiv:1701.01717).
